obligations.rule: Validate URLs with default options.
obligations.why: Ensure basic functionality of the validator with default settings.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with custom protocols.
obligations.why: Test the `protocols` option to ensure only specified protocols are accepted.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `require_protocol` set to true.
obligations.why: Ensure URLs without protocols are rejected when `require_protocol` is enabled.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `require_host` set to false.
obligations.why: Ensure URLs without hosts are accepted when `require_host` is disabled.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `allow_underscores` set to true.
obligations.why: Ensure URLs with underscores in the host are accepted when `allow_underscores` is enabled.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `allow_trailing_dot` set to true.
obligations.why: Ensure URLs with trailing dots in the host are accepted when `allow_trailing_dot` is enabled.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `allow_protocol_relative_urls` set to true.
obligations.why: Ensure protocol-relative URLs are accepted when `allow_protocol_relative_urls` is enabled.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `allow_fragments` set to false.
obligations.why: Ensure URLs with fragments are rejected when `allow_fragments` is disabled.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `allow_query_components` set to false.
obligations.why: Ensure URLs with query components are rejected when `allow_query_components` is disabled.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `disallow_auth` set to true.
obligations.why: Ensure URLs with authentication components are rejected when `disallow_auth` is enabled.

obligations.rule: Validate URLs with `validate_length` set to false.
obligations.why: Ensure URLs exceeding the maximum length are accepted when `validate_length` is disabled.

obligations.rule: Trigger early return for empty or invalid strings.
obligations.why: Ensure the function correctly rejects empty strings and strings with invalid characters.

obligations.rule: Trigger early return for `mailto:` URLs.
obligations.why: Ensure `mailto:` URLs are explicitly rejected.

obligations.rule: Test protocol parsing logic with valid and invalid protocols.
obligations.why: Ensure the function correctly identifies and validates protocols.

obligations.rule: Test port parsing logic with valid and invalid ports.
obligations.why: Ensure the function correctly validates port numbers.

obligations.rule: Test IPv6 parsing logic with valid and invalid IPv6 addresses.
obligations.why: Ensure the function correctly validates IPv6 addresses.

obligations.rule: Test host whitelist and blacklist logic.
obligations.why: Ensure the function correctly validates hosts against whitelist and blacklist rules.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `is_valid_auth && !has_encoded_content` at line 134.
obligations.why: This condition is uncovered and determines whether the input is treated as authentication or a malicious protocol.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `options.require_protocol` at line 136.
obligations.why: This condition is uncovered and affects the validation of authentication components when `require_protocol` is true.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `options.host_blacklist && checkHost(host, options.host_blacklist)` at line 262.
obligations.why: This condition is uncovered and determines whether the host matches the blacklist.

obligations.rule: Cover the uncovered statement at line 59: `return false;` triggered by invalid URLs containing spaces or angle brackets.
obligations.why: This statement is part of the validation logic for rejecting URLs with invalid characters.

obligations.rule: Cover the uncovered statement at line 158: `if (options.require_protocol) { return false; }` triggered when `looks_like_port` is true and `require_protocol` is enabled.
obligations.why: This statement ensures URLs resembling hostname:port are rejected when `require_protocol` is required.

obligations.rule: Cover the uncovered statement at line 188: `return false;` triggered when `had_explicit_protocol` is false and `allow_protocol_relative_urls` is disabled.
obligations.why: This statement ensures protocol-relative URLs are rejected when the option is disabled.

obligations.rule: Cover the uncovered statement at line 67: `return false;`.
obligations.why: This statement is reached when `options.validate_length` is true and the URL length exceeds `options.max_allowed_length`.

obligations.rule: Cover the uncovered statement at line 201: `return true;`.
obligations.why: This statement is reached when the URL is empty and `options.require_host` is false.

obligations.rule: Cover the uncovered statement at line 211: `return false;`.
obligations.why: This statement is reached when the split array's first element is empty, indicating an invalid URL structure.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `url === '' && !options.require_host` at line 201.
obligations.why: Uncovered condition at line 201 checks if an empty URL is valid when `require_host` is disabled.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `split[0] === ''` at line 210.
obligations.why: Uncovered condition at line 210 checks if the first element of the split array is empty.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `auth.indexOf(':') >= 0 && auth.split(':').length > 2` at line 214.
obligations.why: Uncovered condition at line 214 checks for invalid authentication format with excessive colons.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `url === '' && !options.require_host` at line 201.
obligations.why: This condition checks if an empty URL is valid when `require_host` is disabled.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `split[0] === ''` at line 210.
obligations.why: This condition checks for invalid URL structures where the first split element is empty.

obligations.rule: Cover the condition `user === '' && password === ''` at line 218.
obligations.why: This condition checks for invalid authentication components where both user and password are empty.

test_groups.title: Default Options
test_groups.rationale: Test basic URL validation with default settings.

test_groups.title: Custom Protocols
test_groups.rationale: Test validation with custom protocols.

test_groups.title: Require Protocol
test_groups.rationale: Test validation when protocol is required.

test_groups.title: Allow Underscores
test_groups.rationale: Test validation when underscores are allowed in the host.

test_groups.title: Allow Trailing Dot
test_groups.rationale: Test validation when trailing dots are allowed in the host.

test_groups.title: Allow Protocol Relative URLs
test_groups.rationale: Test validation when protocol-relative URLs are allowed.

test_groups.title: Disallow Fragments
test_groups.rationale: Test validation when fragments are disallowed.

test_groups.title: Disallow Query Components
test_groups.rationale: Test validation when query components are disallowed.

test_groups.title: Disallow Authentication
test_groups.rationale: Test validation when authentication components are disallowed.

test_groups.title: Validate Length
test_groups.rationale: Test validation when length validation is disabled.

test_groups.title: Protocol Parsing
test_groups.rationale: Test protocol parsing logic.

test_groups.rationale: Test port parsing logic.

test_groups.title: IPv6 Parsing
test_groups.rationale: Test IPv6 parsing logic.

test_groups.title: Host Whitelist and Blacklist
test_groups.rationale: Test host whitelist and blacklist logic.

test_groups.title: Authentication and Malicious Protocols
test_groups.rationale: Test the condition `is_valid_auth && !has_encoded_content` to distinguish authentication from malicious protocols.

test_groups.title: Require Protocol with Authentication
test_groups.rationale: Test the condition `options.require_protocol` when authentication components are present.

test_groups.title: Host Blacklist Validation
test_groups.rationale: Test the condition `options.host_blacklist && checkHost(host, options.host_blacklist)` to ensure hosts in the blacklist are rejected.

test_groups.title: Reject URLs with Invalid Characters
test_groups.rationale: Test rejection of URLs containing spaces or angle brackets.

test_groups.title: Require Protocol with Looks-Like-Port URLs
test_groups.rationale: Test rejection of URLs resembling hostname:port when `require_protocol` is enabled.

test_groups.title: Disallow Protocol-Relative URLs
test_groups.rationale: Test rejection of protocol-relative URLs when the option is disabled.

test_groups.title: Validate Length Exceeds Maximum
test_groups.rationale: Test URL length validation when `validate_length` is enabled and the URL exceeds the maximum allowed length.

test_groups.title: Empty URL with Require Host Disabled
test_groups.rationale: Test validation of empty URLs when `require_host` is disabled.

test_groups.title: Invalid URL Structure with Empty Split Element
test_groups.rationale: Test validation of URLs with an invalid structure where the split array's first element is empty.

test_groups.title: Empty URL with Require Host Disabled
test_groups.rationale: Test validation of empty URLs when `require_host` is disabled.

test_groups.title: Invalid URL Structure with Empty Split Element
test_groups.rationale: Test validation of URLs with an invalid structure where the split array's first element is empty.

test_groups.title: Invalid Authentication Format
test_groups.rationale: Test validation of URLs with invalid authentication format containing excessive colons.

test_groups.title: Empty URL with Require Host Disabled
test_groups.rationale: Test validation of empty URLs when `require_host` is disabled.

test_groups.title: Invalid URL Structure with Empty Split Element
test_groups.rationale: Test validation of URLs with an invalid structure where the split array's first element is empty.

test_groups.title: Invalid Authentication Format with Empty User and Password
test_groups.rationale: Test validation of URLs with invalid authentication components where both user and password are empty.
